steps.title: Find the sentence boundary
steps.content: The blank separates the complete sentence "Jamaican British artist Willard Wigan is known for his remarkable microsculptures" from a new sentence beginning with a modifying noun phrase about creations so small they are best viewed through a microscope.

steps.title: Reject fused clauses
steps.content: Choice A fuses the two sentences without punctuation. Choice B creates a comma splice, and Choice D uses "and" without the punctuation needed to join complete sentences. The remaining option uses a period to mark the boundary. The answer is C.
